title: Containers in a pod don't share the same network namespace, unless...
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Containers in a pod don't share the same network namespace

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run -dt --rm --pod new:mypod quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx

2. podman run -ti --rm --pod mypod quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx curl localhost

**Describe the results you received:**

> curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 80: Connection refused

**Describe the results you expected:**

> podman rulez

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Creating the pod manually solves the issue. https://podman.io/getting-started/network.html doesn't make this as a requirement.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.0
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.20-1.1.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: unknown'
  cpus: 8
  distribution:
    distribution: '"opensuse-tumbleweed"'
    version: "20200818"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: rivendell
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 100
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.7.11-1-default
  linkmode: dynamic

  os: linux

  rootless: true

      commit: unknown
      libslirp: 4.3.1
  swapFree: 10737414144

  uptime: 29m 37.57s
registries:

  - registry.opensuse.org
  - docker.io

  imageStore:
    number: 2
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers

  APIVersion: 1

  BuiltTime: Tue Aug 11 21:00:00 2020
  GitCommit: ""

  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.0.4
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical

comments.body:
This should be fixed on master. We're cutting a 2.0.5 on Monday which will
include the fix.
